title: Fallback Test Bibliography
intro: A three-entry throwaway bibliography used to test how biblioviewer behaves in a sandbox with no outbound network access (claude.ai, Cowork). Not a real research product — the annotations are filler.

The three sources are chosen for structural variety, not subject matter: one heavily marked-up reference page, one long-form scholarly essay, one catalog record. If reader-mode extraction survives the sandbox, all three should look different from each other in the reading pane.

entries.title: Annotated bibliography
entries.annotation: The highest-signal entry in this set. Wikipedia pages carry headings, footnote superscripts, internal links, italics, and list markup — so this is the entry that reveals whether the sandbox preserved HTML structure or flattened everything to plain prose.

entries.title: Rights
entries.annotation: A long scholarly essay with numbered sections and blockquotes. Tests whether extraction holds up over a document far longer than a typical news article, and whether section hierarchy survives.

entries.title: Pride and Prejudice, by Jane Austen
entries.annotation: A catalog record rather than an article — mostly a metadata table with download links. Readability often struggles with pages like this, so it doubles as a test of the graceful-failure path.
